nodes.label: Web Search: Moving to Serper (and dropping DuckDuckGo)
nodes.norm_label: web search: moving to serper (and dropping duckduckgo)

nodes.label: What the fast assistants do (and why Serper fits)
nodes.norm_label: what the fast assistants do (and why serper fits)

nodes.label: OpenAI-compatible Provider System
nodes.rationale: One provider system means a single API key works for assistant answers, dictation post-processing, and remote TTS.

nodes.label: Manager Pattern
nodes.rationale: Core functionality is organized into managers (Audio, Model, Transcription) initialized at startup and shared via Tauri state.

nodes.label: Paste Safety Net
nodes.rationale: Synthetic-paste flows always release modifiers after a key combo so an interrupted paste can never leave modifiers stuck pressed at the OS level.

nodes.label: ONNX Runtime Dynamic Link (Intel Mac)
nodes.rationale: Prebuilt ONNX Runtime binaries are not available for Intel Macs, so install via Homebrew and link dynamically using ORT_LIB_LOCATION and ORT_PREFER_DYNAMIC_LINK.

nodes.label: AppImage linuxdeploy strip Issue
nodes.rationale: linuxdeploy bundles an old strip binary that cannot process libraries from newer toolchains on rolling-release distros; deb/rpm/binary bundles build fine, so skip the AppImage step.

nodes.label: Community Feedback Priority
nodes.rationale: PRs with demonstrated community interest are much more likely to be merged; discussion-first prevents feature creep and bloat.

nodes.label: Deferred Translation Status
nodes.rationale: English is the source and the app changes fast, so translation into other locales is batched into one pass at the end rather than chasing every UI change.

nodes.label: Keep i18n Keys as 'characters'
nodes.rationale: Only displayed values change to 'Personas'; renaming the internal i18n keys would touch code and every locale for no user benefit.

nodes.label: One Color Moment Principle
nodes.rationale: Color appears as a rare deliberate accent against warm paper and ink, never as wall-to-wall chrome.

nodes.label: Secure API-Key Storage in OS Keychain
nodes.norm_label: secure api-key storage in os keychain

nodes.label: STT Recording Overlay
nodes.rationale: The clean, minimal dark content-hugging pill is the visual north star the assistant window should match.

nodes.label: Assistant Panel
nodes.rationale: Currently too big, inconsistent, light by default and janky; rebuilt to be the STT overlay's calmer sibling.

nodes.label: AudioWaveform Component
nodes.rationale: Shared component to reuse, not rebuild.

nodes.label: Voice HUD Pill
nodes.rationale: Voice-only collapsed default state, as small and calm as the STT overlay.

nodes.label: Sticky Screen Vision
nodes.rationale: Persist screen-vision arming until toggled instead of one-shot consumption per turn.

nodes.label: Opacity Bug Fix
nodes.rationale: The 0.97 alpha cap on --panel-surface means the surface is never fully opaque; max opacity must be truly opaque.

nodes.label: Customization Removal
nodes.rationale: Remove 6 accent colors, accent orb glow, opacity slider and size presets; keep only light/dark and optional text size to stay calm.

nodes.label: Error Feedback Requirement
nodes.rationale: Every user-hittable failure must surface as a short plain-language localized message; no silent no-ops, endless spinners, or raw dumps.

nodes.label: Region Screenshot Capture
nodes.rationale: Let the user draw a box to screenshot just part of the screen, with full-screen fallback, triggered from the existing capture control.

nodes.label: Phased Delivery with GATE Checks
nodes.rationale: Work phase by phase, stopping at each GATE for a human build and visual check before continuing.

nodes.label: graphify Knowledge Graph
nodes.rationale: Use the graphify graph to learn the codebase before editing.

nodes.label: Generated bindings.ts
nodes.rationale: Never hand-edit; it is generated by tauri-specta on build.

nodes.label: i18n Everything Requirement
nodes.rationale: No hardcoded user-facing strings in JSX; ESLint enforces this.

nodes.label: Backward-compatible Settings
nodes.rationale: Use serde default and tolerate legacy keys so existing stored settings still load; never wipe user settings.

nodes.label: SCREENSHOT_MARKER
nodes.rationale: Appended to stored user messages and stripped for display; both sides must stay in sync.

nodes.label: Atmospheric Gradient Orbs
nodes.rationale: Brand voltage is photographic not chromatic; pastel orbs are the only color moments, used as decoration only, never as fills or text.

nodes.label: Waldenburg Light Display
nodes.rationale: Display serif at weight 300 is the editorial signature; never bolded.

nodes.label: Ink Pill CTA
nodes.rationale: Near-black ink pill is the only CTA color; no saturated brand action color.

nodes.label: Editorial Magazine Aesthetic
nodes.rationale: The brand trusts atmospheric photography and modest type weights to do brand work; quiet print-magazine feel, no neon accent or dev-tools dark canvas.

nodes.label: Font Substitutes (EB Garamond / GT Sectra)
nodes.rationale: Waldenburg is licensed; EB Garamond weight 300 or GT Sectra are documented open-source substitutes.

nodes.label: Answer Box
nodes.rationale: Google's direct answer, highest value for quick facts, added first.

nodes.label: Top Stories (news)
nodes.rationale: Only pulled when the planner flags the turn as news-y (include_news); flagged from_news for the recency boost.

nodes.label: Snippet-only Search
nodes.rationale: Serper returns snippets only, matching the snippet-first behavior; full-page fetching removed in Phase 2.0.

nodes.label: Provider Dispatch (snippet_search)
nodes.rationale: Unknown/legacy provider values route to serper so old settings keep working.

nodes.label: Snippet Provider Set
nodes.rationale: Serper (default), Brave, Tavily, Exa, SerpAPI, all single-key with a free tier.

nodes.label: Secret Store Key (web_search:serper)
nodes.rationale: account_web_search(provider_id) is generic so the key stores under web_search:serper automatically.

nodes.label: Graceful Degradation
nodes.rationale: Missing key or non-200 is swallowed per-query and logged; the turn answers without web context rather than breaking.

nodes.label: round_robin_merge Dedupe
nodes.rationale: Duplicate URLs across answer box / knowledge graph / organic are de-duplicated downstream, keyed on URL.

nodes.label: Verification Checklist
nodes.rationale: Run cargo check --tests (not just check), grep across every file type, and use the real Test-search path before declaring a provider change done.

nodes.label: Firecrawl Removal (Phase 2.0)
nodes.rationale: Firecrawl, full-page scraping, the credit guard, and fetch_content/daily_credit_budget UI were dropped; settings/commands remain as deprecated no-ops for back-compat.

nodes.label: DuckDuckGo Removal
nodes.rationale: Keyless HTML-scraping endpoint parsed with brittle regexes, low quality and easily rate-limited; added no value once Serper is default, so removed to keep the provider set tight.

nodes.label: Snippet-first Retrieval
nodes.rationale: Fast assistants answer from ranked snippets/passages, not full-page dumps; the correct answer is very often already in the one-line snippet.

nodes.label: Top-N Character Cropping Problem
nodes.rationale: Cropping the top N characters of a scraped page usually captures navigation and boilerplate, not the answering sentence.

nodes.label: Excessive Context Problem
nodes.rationale: Feeding a small local model a big noisy blob is slower and less accurate, and costs more tokens.

nodes.label: Confident-but-stale Failure
nodes.rationale: When search returned nothing the turn answered from stale training data and got current facts wrong.

nodes.label: Perplexity RAG Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Multi-stage RAG over its own index with hybrid retrieval, a multi-layer reranker, and citations wired in before the model writes.

nodes.label: Cross-encoder Reranker
nodes.rationale: The missing piece versus Perplexity; a rerank over merged candidates improves accuracy and shrinks the prompt.

nodes.label: Bing Search API Retirement
nodes.rationale: Bing Search API was retired Aug 11 2025, so a third-party SERP API like Serper is the practical equivalent.

nodes.label: Serper Cost / Free Tier
nodes.rationale: ~2,500 free credits, ~$0.30-$1.00 per 1,000 queries; the earlier 10-20 credit worry was a misread of the old Firecrawl scrape cost.

nodes.label: Fail-safe Grounding
nodes.rationale: If the best reranked score is weak, say sources weren't found or re-query; never silently fall back to training-data memory.

nodes.label: SearXNG Provider (planned)
nodes.rationale: Free self-hosted metasearch, no API key, fits the project's offline-friendly philosophy.

nodes.label: Auto-migration of Legacy Settings
nodes.rationale: Users whose settings say duckduckgo are auto-migrated to the new default on load; any unknown value routes to Serper so nothing breaks.

nodes.label: Rust Code Style
nodes.rationale: Use anyhow::Error with descriptive messages, Arc<Mutex<T>> for shared manager state, builder pattern, snake_case fns / PascalCase types.

nodes.label: TypeScript/React Code Style
nodes.rationale: Functional components with TypeScript interfaces, Zod schemas, useCallback for stable refs, prefer interface aliases and named imports.

nodes.label: Error Handling Conventions
nodes.rationale: Frontend try/catch with user feedback and optimistic rollback; backend ? operator with anyhow context messages.

nodes.label: Component Patterns
nodes.rationale: Container component pattern for layout, composition over inheritance, context to minimize prop drilling.

nodes.label: Assistant Window Entry Point
nodes.rationale: Transparent shell where the React app owns per-mode layout: the pill floats centered; the expanded panel adds its own shadow gutter.

nodes.label: Pill Preview Harness
nodes.rationale: Static preview page mimicking the transparent 232x64 pill window plus the #root 10px gutter, with light/dark backdrops for eyeballing.

nodes.label: Snip (Region Screenshot) Window
nodes.rationale: Transparent full-window crosshair-cursor surface for drawing a region selection.

nodes.label: GigaAM Russian Vocabulary
nodes.rationale: BPE subword vocabulary (Cyrillic-heavy, ~257 tokens incl. <unk>/<blk>) for the GigaAM speech-to-text model.
